expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: TDD-logsql-cli.md exists in outputs/ directory

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Word count is 4889 words, well above the 1500 threshold

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Problem & context' present, discussing INC-4421 and DX survey

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section '2. Scope' explicitly lists 'In scope (v1)' and 'Out of scope (explicit, with reason)'

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '3. High-level approach' with one-paragraph summary and ASCII diagram

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section '4. Detailed design' covers command surface, exit codes, STDIN, memory/streaming, schema, parser, error reporting, dependencies

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section '5. Alternatives considered' lists Go vs Python vs Rust, DuckDB vs SQLite vs custom vs DataFusion, schema strategy, parser strictness, output format, do-nothing — each with rejection rationale

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section 6 with targets like '< 5 s on a 1 GB gzipped input', '≤ 512 MB RSS', '4 GB' default max memory, 'p95 wall-clock... regresses > 20%'

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section '7. Risks & failure modes' with a detailed table of risks, likelihood, mitigation, recovery

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' section appropriate for a CLI (release / distribution / versioning)
expectations.evidence: Section '8. Rollout & migration' covers Homebrew, GitHub Release, semver, phased adoption (alpha/dogfood/GA)

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section '10. Open questions' contains 10 numbered open questions

expectations.text: No fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems
expectations.evidence: No invented internal URLs; only references to incident IDs (INC-4421), Sigstore cosign, CycloneDB, and channels like #devtools/#oncall

expectations.text: TDD is structured as a CLI utility design
expectations.evidence: Header explicitly states 'Workload category: CLI utility'; design centers on command surface, exit codes, STDIN, distribution

expectations.text: Sketches the command surface with flags / args / --help shape
expectations.evidence: Section 4a includes a full sketched --help with Usage, Arguments, Flags (--format, --schema-hint, --max-memory, --temp-dir, --explain, etc.), and Exit codes

expectations.text: Defines exit codes (incl. any tool-specific codes as a deliberate design choice)
expectations.evidence: Section 4b defines exit codes 0-5 with table; explicit design stance to NOT overload exit code with 'no rows found' unlike grep

expectations.text: Documents STDIN / pipe composition behavior (reads stdin when no files; works in pipelines)
expectations.evidence: Section 4c: 'cat file.jsonl | logsql' works; 'Interactive TTY with no input → exit 2'; mixed stdin + files documented

expectations.text: Explicitly states streaming vs whole-load posture, with memory bound for aggregations (GROUP BY / ORDER BY)
expectations.evidence: Section 4d: 'Non-aggregating queries: ≤ 512 MB resident'; 'Aggregating queries: ≤ --max-memory resident (default 4 GB); spills to disk beyond'

expectations.text: Specifies output formats and how the user selects between them (--json/--csv/--tsv/etc.)
expectations.evidence: '-f, --format <json|csv|tsv|table>' flag with TTY-aware default (json when piped, table when TTY)

expectations.text: Takes an explicit position on Go vs Python with stated tradeoffs (does not punt to 'either is fine')
expectations.evidence: Section 5: 'Chosen: Go' with reasoning — single static binary, broken python toolchains, cross-compilation, DuckDB wheel size; Python rejected explicitly

expectations.text: Takes an explicit position on SQL parser strictness with reasoning
expectations.evidence: Section 4f: 'Decision: hand DuckDB the SQL... Strict by DuckDB's standards, not by ANSI's; no forgiving mode' with stated reasons against lenient parsing

expectations.text: 'Alternatives considered' includes existing tools like jq, duckdb, awk, or similar — with honest rejection rationale
expectations.evidence: Section 5 explicitly compares DuckDB (chosen) vs SQLite vs custom vs Apache DataFusion; also Do-nothing alternative discussing status quo jq|grep|awk chains

expectations.text: Explicitly omits NFRs that do not apply to a CLI (availability SLO, multi-region, disaster recovery) — states the omission rather than fabricating values
expectations.evidence: Section 6 has 'NFRs explicitly omitted' subsection: 'Availability SLO: N/A — it's a CLI'; 'Disaster recovery: N/A — no persistent state'; 'Multi-region: N/A — local tool'

expectations.text: Addresses the 5s/1GB-compressed performance target with a real plan
expectations.evidence: Performance NFR: 'Target: time-to-first-result < 5 s on a 1 GB gzipped input on 2024-era M-series MBP'; mechanism is DuckDB streaming scan with vectorised execution; verification via 'make bench' nightly with > 20% regression failure
